assumptions: No Checkly account, CLI authentication, project configuration, checks, alert channels, deployment, or dashboard resources are assumed.
An existing application project, terminal, Node.js 20.19 or newer or 22.12 or newer, npm, browser, and internet connection are available.
No package was installed, file or dependency was changed, prompt was given to an agent, browser signup was submitted, token was stored, check was run, or resource was deployed during research.

developer_goal: Start without a Checkly account, add Monitoring as Code to an existing project with the vendor-recommended quickstart, authenticate, test the generated checks, deploy them, and verify that the checks are live in the Checkly dashboard.

first_success.outcome: The project's generated or edited checks pass a Checkly cloud test, are deployed, and appear in the dashboard ready for scheduled monitoring.
first_success.observable_signal: The manual quickstart shows two passing checks after checkly test, then says the dashboard shows the deployed checks ready to start monitoring after checkly deploy; the recommended route tells the developer to open the dashboard to see monitoring live.

route_selection.surface: Checkly Monitoring as Code quickstart with deployed checks
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Checkly Monitoring as Code quickstart with deployed checks
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Manual Monitoring as Code setup
candidates.reason: This is the fully enumerated fallback on the same page, while the vendor explicitly labels the AI-agent route recommended and fastest.

candidates.name: Dashboard-created checks
candidates.reason: The current general Quickstart selects Monitoring as Code in an existing project rather than a dashboard-only flow.

candidates.name: Playwright test import
candidates.reason: This is a specialized route for projects with existing Playwright tests.

required_path.action: Complete Checkly account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Checkly account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Checkly candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The manual quickstart shows two passing checks after checkly test, then says the dashboard shows the deployed checks ready to start monitoring after checkly deploy; the recommended route tells the developer to open the dashboard to see monitoring live.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'environment', 'requirement': 'Have an existing project directory where Monitoring as Code can be added.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'software', 'requirement': 'Install Node.js 20.19 or newer or 22.12 or newer and npm, and have a terminal.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'software', 'requirement': 'Have a supported AI coding agent for the recommended route; otherwise use the documented manual route.', 'required': False, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: The quickstart assumes an existing application project.
Node.js in a documented version range, npm, Checkly CLI dependencies, and project files are required.
The recommended flow requires choosing an AI coding agent, while manual setup follows a different prompt branch.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Deploy synthetic monitoring checks from the project's Checkly configuration.

excluded.item: Additional checks, alert channels, CI/CD, private locations, dashboards, status pages, incident management, root-cause features, and destroying resources occur after the first checks are deployed and
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact files, checks, alert channels, and prompts will the recommended agent route produce for a particular project?
uncertainties.impact: The official recommended path cannot be reduced to one deterministic atomic sequence.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The quickstart explicitly says the agent's actions depend on the project and lists possible, not universal, actions.

uncertainties.question: What fields, validation, identity alternatives, and verification steps appear during new-user registration?
uncertainties.impact: The mandatory no-account browser segment cannot be recorded atomically.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The CLI docs say Complete account registration and the live application is client-rendered with no readable form detail.

uncertainties.question: Does the 5-minute claim cover the recommended agent route, the manual route, or both under all prerequisites?
uncertainties.impact: The claim should not be treated as a measured duration for this reconstructed no-account journey.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The docs home states the claim but does not publish its conditions or measurement method.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Checkly show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
